items.title: Trees & BSTs
items.description: Most tree algorithms are recursive: 'do something at the root, recurse into children'.

items.title: Graphs
items.description: DFS, cycle detection and topological sort are recursive in their classical form (and iterative for deep graphs).

items.title: Stacks & Queues
items.description: Every recursion is implicitly using the call stack; an explicit stack converts it into a loop.

items.title: Dynamic Programming
items.description: Top-down DP IS memoized recursion; bottom-up DP is the iterative companion.

items.title: Sorting Algorithms
items.description: Mergesort and quicksort are textbook divide-and-conquer recursions.

items.title: Time-Space Tradeoffs
items.description: Memoization buys speed (O(2ⁿ) → O(n)) at the cost of O(n) memory — a clean tradeoff.
